caption_stub: Exact Var[k] vs mode number M for several squeezing/loss settings, showing exponential concentration of the Gaussian fidelity kernel (gaussian_concentration)
Per-mode concentration rate c vs transmissivity eta for three squeezing values; rate decreases with loss, falsifying H1's stated direction (rate_vs_loss)
Displacement scale required to restore the lossless bandwidth vs eta; loss-induced concentration is exactly bandwidth-reversible in the Gaussian sector, falsifying H3 (bandwidth_compensation)
(a) Var[k] vs M for the GBS feature kernel vs the Gaussian kernel; (b) GBS Var[k] vs eta at M=3. GBS concentrates more slowly than Gaussian at small M (gbs_concentration)

Concentration rate c (left) and Wigner negativity N (right) vs eta for the squeezed-single-photon kernel at (a) r=0.5 and (b) r=1.0; the simulability threshold (N->0, shaded) and the smooth nonzero concentration rate diverge (h4_boundary)

Exact Var[k] vs M for the non-Gaussian product kernel at three loss levels (one classically simulable), showing exponential concentration persists into the simulable regime (nongaussian_concentration)
